channel.title: AI Unfiltered
channel.description: Chinese AI • Open Source • Security • Incidents. Signal, not noise.

item.title: Weibo Launches AI-Only Community Where Humans Can Only Observe
item.description: Weibo launches an AI-only social space where bots interact freely while humans watch from the sidelines.

item.title: Hesai Reports RMB 3B+ Revenue in 2025, Triples Shipments to Become First LiDAR Firm with Full-Year GAAP Profit
item.description: Hesai becomes the first LiDAR company to achieve full-year GAAP profitability, driven by record revenue and tripled shipments.

item.title: Momenta Files Confidentially for Hong Kong IPO, Targets Listing Within 2026
item.description: Momenta targets a 2026 Hong Kong IPO at a valuation above USD 14 billion, underscoring growing momentum in China’s autonomous driving sector.

item.title: Unable to download or preview project files
item.description: Status: InvestigatingWe are investigating the issue for the listed services.Affected components File uploads (Degraded performance) Files (Operational)

item.title: Disruption with some GitHub services
item.description: Mar 24, 20:38 UTCUpdate - We are investigating elevated error rates affecting multiple GitHub services including Actions, Issues, Pull Requests, Webhooks, Codespaces, and login functionality. Some users may have experienced errors when accessing these features. Most services are now showing signs...

item.title: Elevated error rates on Realtime API SIP endpoints
item.description: Status: ResolvedAll impacted services have now fully recovered.Affected components Realtime (Operational)

item.title: DoE Publishes 5-Year Energy Security Plan
item.description: CESER’s Project Armor is a five year initiative to harden the US critical energy infrastructure, including strengthening energy systems ‘to prevent and recover from wildfires and other hazards’. The post DoE Publishes 5-Year Energy Security Plan appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Network Performance Issues in Osaka, Japan datacenter (KIX)
item.description: Mar 24, 18:32 UTCResolved - This incident has been resolved.Mar 24, 18:13 UTCIdentified - Cloudflare is investigating issues with Network Performance in Osaka, Japan datacenter (KIX).We are working to analyze and mitigate this problem. More updates to follow shortly.

item.title: Why Agentic AI Systems Need Better Governance – Lessons from OpenClaw
item.description: Agentic AI platforms are shifting from passive recommendation tools to autonomous action-takers with real system access, The post Why Agentic AI Systems Need Better Governance – Lessons from OpenClaw appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: TeamPCP Backdoors LiteLLM Versions 1.82.7–1.82.8 Likely via Trivy CI/CD Compromise
item.description: TeamPCP, the threat actor behind the recent compromises of Trivy and KICS, has now compromised a popular Python package named litellm, pushing two malicious versions containing a credential harvester, a Kubernetes lateral movement toolkit, and a persistent backdoor. Multiple security vendors,...

item.title: Exclusive eBook: Are we ready to hand AI agents the keys?
item.description: We’re starting to give AI agents real autonomy, but are we prepared for what could happen next? This subscriber-only eBook explores this and angles from experts, such as “If we continue on the current path … we are basically playing Russian roulette with humanity.” by Grace Huckins June 12, 2025...

item.title: Cloudflare API service issues
item.description: Mar 24, 18:15 UTCMonitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results.Mar 24, 17:59 UTCUpdate - The Cloudflare Workers and GraphQL API is experiencing increased errors and changes to Cron Triggers may be delayed. Already deployed Workers and existing Cron Triggers are...

item.title: Poland Faced a Surge in Cyberattacks in 2025, Including a Major Assault on the Energy Sector
item.description: The attacks included a destructive infiltration of Poland's energy system in December and was suspected of originating in Russia. The post Poland Faced a Surge in Cyberattacks in 2025, Including a Major Assault on the Energy Sector appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Tax Search Ads Deliver ScreenConnect Malware Using Huawei Driver to Disable EDR
item.description: A large-scale malvertising campaign active since January 2026 has been observed targeting U.S.-based individuals searching for tax-related documents to serve rogue installers for ConnectWise ScreenConnect that drop a tool named HwAudKiller to blind security programs using the bring your own...

item.title: Teams Github Notifications App is down
item.description: Mar 24, 16:59 UTCInvestigating - We are investigating reports of impacted performance for some GitHub services.

item.title: 🔬Why There Is No "AlphaFold for Materials" — AI for Materials Discovery with Heather Kulik
item.description: Lessons from a Decade on the Frontier of AI for Science

item.title: This scientist rewarmed and studied pieces of his friend’s cryopreserved brain
item.description: L. Stephen Coles’s brain sits cushioned in a vat at a storage facility in Arizona. It has been held there at a temperature of around −146 degrees °C for over a decade, largely undisturbed. That is, apart from the time, a little over a year ago, when scientists slowly lifted the brain to take...

item.title: Schema Validation Block Reason Not Displayed on Dashboard
item.description: Mar 24, 16:42 UTCIdentified - The issue has been identified and a fix is being implemented.Mar 24, 16:10 UTCInvestigating - Customers may notice that when a request is blocked by schema validation, the associated reason or description is not being displayed within the dashboard.

item.title: Hackers Use Fake Resumes to Steal Enterprise Credentials and Deploy Crypto Miner
item.description: An ongoing phishing campaign is targeting French-speaking corporate environments with fake resumes that lead to the deployment of cryptocurrency miners and information stealers. "The campaign uses highly obfuscated VBScript files disguised as resume/CV documents, delivered through phishing emails,"...

item.title: RSAC 2026 Conference Announcements Summary (Day 1)
item.description: A summary of the announcements made by vendors on the first day of the RSAC 2026 Conference. The post RSAC 2026 Conference Announcements Summary (Day 1) appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Extortion Group Claims It Hacked AstraZeneca
item.description: The Lapsus$ hackers allegedly compromised internal code repositories, credentials, and employee data. The post Extortion Group Claims It Hacked AstraZeneca appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: The Download: tracing AI-fueled delusions, and OpenAI admits Microsoft risks
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. The hardest question to answer about AI-fueled delusions  What actually happens when people spiral into delusion with AI? To find...

item.title: Increased errors creating new sync apps and sync delays with existing apps
item.description: Status: ResolvedAll impacted services have now fully recovered.Affected components Connectors/Apps (Operational)

item.title: The Hidden Cost of Cybersecurity Specialization: Losing Foundational Skills
item.description: Cybersecurity has changed fast. Roles are more specialized, and tooling is more advanced. On paper, this should make organizations more secure. But in practice, many teams struggle with the same basic problems they faced years ago: unclear risk priorities, misaligned tooling decisions, and...

item.title: Ghost Campaign Uses 7 npm Packages to Steal Crypto Wallets and Credentials
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have uncovered a new set of malicious npm packages that are designed to steal cryptocurrency wallets and sensitive data. The activity is being tracked by ReversingLabs as the Ghost campaign. The list of identified packages, all published by a user named mikilanjillo, is...

item.title: NCSC CEO: Seize 'disruptive' vibe coding opportunity to make software more secure
item.description: Dr Richard Horne delivered a keynote about cyber risks and opportunities at the RSAC Conference in San Francisco

item.title: Vibe check: AI may replace SaaS (but not for a while)
item.description: If ‘vibe coding’ disrupts the software market like SaaS did 20 years ago, what does this mean for cyber security?

item.title: Alibaba DAMO Academy Launches XuanTie C950 CPU for Large AI Models
item.description: Alibaba DAMO Academy has unveiled the XuanTie C950, a RISC-V CPU achieving over 70 points on the SPECint2006 benchmark — the highest single-core performance ever recorded for the architecture — with native support for billion-parameter LLMs like Qwen3 and DeepSeek V3.

item.title: MRTIS TechBio Secures Approval for Hong Kong IPO Filing
item.description: Metacell Therapeutics, an AI-driven nanomedicine platform company founded by MIT scientists and an NAE member, has received regulatory approval for its Hong Kong IPO after raising over RMB 2.5 billion from investors including Sequoia and China Life.

item.title: [AINews] Dreamer joins Meta Superintelligence Labs — 9 month retro of Personal Superintelligence
item.description: By now we’re pretty used to LS Pod guests going on to great success, but today’s news is fast for even us - Nat and Alex at MSL have execuhired Dreamer just days after we shipped their pod, barely 11 days after we recorded with them:

item.title: Some customer may encounter errors creating, updating projects, and uploading files
item.description: Status: InvestigatingWe are investigating the issue for the listed services.Affected components File uploads (Degraded performance)

item.title: AgenticGEO: A Self-Evolving Agentic System for Generative Engine Optimization
item.description: arXiv:2603.20213v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Generative search engines represent a transition from traditional ranking-based retrieval to Large Language Model (LLM)-based synthesis, transforming optimization goals from ranking prominence towards content inclusion. Generative Engine Optimization...

item.title: ProMAS: Proactive Error Forecasting for Multi-Agent Systems Using Markov Transition Dynamics
item.description: arXiv:2603.20260v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The integration of Large Language Models into Multi-Agent Systems (MAS) has enabled the so-lution of complex, long-horizon tasks through collaborative reasoning. However, this collec-tive intelligence is inherently fragile, as a single logical fallacy...

item.title: Domain-Specialized Tree of Thought through Plug-and-Play Predictors
item.description: arXiv:2603.20267v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: While Large Language Models (LLMs) have advanced complex reasoning, prominent methods like the Tree of Thoughts (ToT) framework face a critical trade-off between exploration depth and computational efficiency. Existing ToT implementations often rely...

item.title: FactorSmith: Agentic Simulation Generation via Markov Decision Process Decomposition with Planner-Designer-Critic Refinement
item.description: arXiv:2603.20270v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Generating executable simulations from natural language specifications remains a challenging problem due to the limited reasoning capacity of large language models (LLMs) when confronted with large, interconnected codebases. This paper presents...

item.title: Me, Myself, and $\pi$ : Evaluating and Explaining LLM Introspection
item.description: arXiv:2603.20276v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: A hallmark of human intelligence is Introspection-the ability to assess and reason about one's own cognitive processes. Introspection has emerged as a promising but contested capability in large language models (LLMs). However, current evaluations...

item.title: JointFM-0.1: A Foundation Model for Multi-Target Joint Distributional Prediction
item.description: arXiv:2603.20266v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Despite the rapid advancements in Artificial Intelligence (AI), Stochastic Differential Equations (SDEs) remain the gold-standard formalism for modeling systems under uncertainty. However, applying SDEs in practice is fraught with challenges: modeling...

item.title: MARLIN: Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning for Incremental DAG Discovery
item.description: arXiv:2603.20295v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Uncovering causal structures from observational data is crucial for understanding complex systems and making informed decisions. While reinforcement learning (RL) has shown promise in identifying these structures in the form of a directed acyclic...

item.title: Collaborative Adaptive Curriculum for Progressive Knowledge Distillation
item.description: arXiv:2603.20296v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Recent advances in collaborative knowledge distillation have demonstrated cutting-edge performance for resource-constrained distributed multimedia learning scenarios. However, achieving such competitiveness requires addressing a fundamental mismatch:...

item.title: Transformer-Based Predictive Maintenance for Risk-Aware Instrument Calibration
item.description: arXiv:2603.20297v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Accurate calibration is essential for instruments whose measurements must remain traceable, reliable, and compliant over long operating periods. Fixed-interval programs are easy to administer, but they ignore that instruments drift at different rates...

item.title: Rolling-Origin Validation Reverses Model Rankings in Multi-Step PM10 Forecasting: XGBoost, SARIMA, and Persistence
item.description: arXiv:2603.20315v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: (a) Many air quality forecasting studies report gains from machine learning, but evaluations often use static chronological splits and omit persistence baselines, so the operational added value under routine updating is unclear. (b) Using 2,350 daily...

item.title: Enhancing Safety of Large Language Models via Embedding Space Separation
item.description: arXiv:2603.20206v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) have achieved impressive capabilities, yet ensuring their safety against harmful prompts remains a critical challenge. Recent work has revealed that the latent representations (embeddings) of harmful and safe queries in...

item.title: RedacBench: Can AI Erase Your Secrets?
item.description: arXiv:2603.20208v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Modern language models can readily extract sensitive information from unstructured text, making redaction -- the selective removal of such information -- critical for data security. However, existing benchmarks for redaction typically focus on...

item.title: Children's Intelligence Tests Pose Challenges for MLLMs? KidGym: A 2D Grid-Based Reasoning Benchmark for MLLMs
item.description: arXiv:2603.20209v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) combine the linguistic strengths of LLMs with the ability to process multimodal data, enbaling them to address a broader range of visual tasks. Because MLLMs aim at more general, human-like competence than...

item.title: CRoCoDiL: Continuous and Robust Conditioned Diffusion for Language
item.description: arXiv:2603.20210v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Masked Diffusion Models (MDMs) provide an efficient non-causal alternative to autoregressive generation but often struggle with token dependencies and semantic incoherence due to their reliance on discrete marginal distributions. We address these...

item.title: Fast-Slow Thinking RM: Efficient Integration of Scalar and Generative Reward Models
item.description: arXiv:2603.20212v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Reward models (RMs) are critical for aligning Large Language Models via Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback (RLHF). While Generative Reward Models (GRMs) achieve superior accuracy through chain-of-thought (CoT) reasoning, they incur substantial...

item.title: Increase in AI Gateway errors
item.description: Mar 24, 02:16 UTCResolved - This incident has been resolved.Mar 24, 02:09 UTCMonitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results.Mar 24, 02:03 UTCInvestigating - Cloudflare is aware of, and investigating an issue which potentially impacts multiple customers. Customers may be...

item.title: A New Framework for Evaluation of Voice Agents (EVA)
Request Trace is failing for some requests
item.description: Mar 23, 20:25 UTCInvestigating - The request trace feature is failing for some cacheable assets. Failing requests are returning 400 Bad Request.

item.title: WorldCache: Content-Aware Caching for Accelerated Video World Models
item.description: Diffusion Transformers (DiTs) power high-fidelity video world models but remain computationally expensive due to sequential denoising and costly spatio-temporal attention. Training-free feature caching accelerates inference by reusing intermediate activations across denoising steps; however,...
